2604.11195.title: Towards Adaptive Open-Set Object Detection via Category-Level Collaboration Knowledge Mining
2604.11195.paper_summary: Existing object detectors often struggle to generalize across domains while adapting to emerging novel categories. Adaptive open-set object detection (AOOD) addresses this challenge by training on base categories in the source domain and adapting to both base and novel categories in the target domain without target annotations. However, current AOOD methods remain limited by weak cross-domain representations, ambiguity among novel categories, and source-domain feature bias. To address these issues, we propose a category-level collaboration knowledge mining strategy that exploits both inter-class and intra-class relationships across domains. Specifically, we construct a clustering-based memory bank to encode class prototypes, auxiliary features, and intra-class disparity information, and iteratively update it via unsupervised clustering to enhance category-level knowledge representation. We further design a base-to-novel selection metric to discover source-domain features related to novel categories and use them to initialize novel-category classifiers. In addition, an adaptive feature assignment strategy transfers the learned category-level knowledge to the target domain and asynchronously updates the memory bank to alleviate source-domain bias. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks show that our method consistently surpasses state-of-the-art AOOD methods by 1.1-5.5 mAP.
2604.11195.paper_summary_zh: 現有的物體檢測器在跨領域泛化以及適應新興類別時常常面臨挑戰。自適應開放集物體檢測（AOOD）通過在源領域上對基礎類別進行訓練，並在目標領域上在沒有目標註釋的情況下適應基礎類別和新類別來應對這一挑戰。然而，目前的AOOD方法仍然受到弱跨領域表示、新類別之間的模糊性以及源領域特徵偏差的限制。為了解決這些問題，我們提出了一種類別級協作知識挖掘策略，利用跨領域的類間和類內關係。具體而言，我們構建了一個基於聚類的記憶庫來編碼類別原型、輔助特徵和類內差異信息，並通過無監督聚類迭代更新它，以增強類別級知識表示。我們進一步設計了一個基於基礎到新類別的選擇度量，以發現與新類別相關的源領域特徵，並用它們來初始化新類別分類器。此外，自適應特徵分配策略將學習到的類別級知識轉移到目標領域，並異步更新記憶庫以減輕源領域偏差。在多個基準上的廣泛實驗顯示，我們的方法在mAP上始終超越最先進的AOOD方法1.1-5.5。
2604.11195.author: Yuqi Ji et.al.
2604.11195.authors: Yuqi Ji, Junjie Ke, Lihuo He, Lizhi Wang, Xinbo Gao
